docket_entries.description: REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE (TRANSACTION ID # 100056701) FILED BY PETITIONER SENIOR AND DISABILITY ACTION CHIU, ALICE AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERTIES UNION OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR PETITIONERS SENIOR AND DISABILITY ACTION, ALICE CHIU, AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERTIES UNION OF NORTHERN'S MOTION FOR A PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE. (SPECIALLY SET AT 1:30 PM BEFORE JUDGE SCHULMAN) HEARING SET FOR FEB-22-2019 AT 01:30 PM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PETITIONERS SENIOR AND DISABILITY ACTION, ALICE CHIU, AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERTIES UNION OF NORTHERN'S MOTION FOR A PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE. (SPECIALLY SET AT 1:30 PM BEFORE JUDGE SCHULMAN) THE COURT PROPOSE TO RECEIVE COMPETING STATEMENTS OF DECISION FROM BOTH COUNSEL (DRAFT STATEMENTS OF DECISION) AND TO USE THEM AS A STARTING POINT FOR ISSUING A WRITTEN RULING. IT WOULD BE VERY USEFUL TO THE COURT IF BOTH COUNSEL COULD SUBMIT TO THE COURT PROPOSED STATEMENTS OF DECISION ADDRESSING THE KEY ISSUES ON WHICH THE RESOLUTION OF THE PETITION TURNS, AND CITING THE KEY AUTHORITIES THAT PARTIES RELY UPON IN THAT REGARD. THE COURT PROPOSES TO REVIEW BOTH COMPETING STATEMENTS OF DECISION, DRAFT STATEMENTS OF DECISION AND TO USE THEM AS A STARTING POINT FOR ISSUING A WRITTEN RULING. THE STATEMENTS OF DECISION SHOULD ADDRESS THE PRINCIPLE MATERIAL ISSUES HERE THAT ARE BEFORE THE COURT FOR PRINCIPAL RESOLUTION. THESE ARE TO BE SUBMITTED SIMULTANEOUSLY AND IN WORD VERSION BY EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG THESE ARE TO BE RECEIVED BY MARCH 8, 2019, AT WHICH TIME THIS WILL BE THEN CONSIDERED TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. (SEE COURT REPORTER TRANSCRIPTS FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) JUDGE: ETAHN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Petitioners Senior And Disability Action, Alice Chiu, American Civil Liberties Union Of Northern'S Motion For A Peremptory Writ Of Mandate. (Specially Set At 1:30 Pm Before Judge Schulman)
Off Calendar Mar-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Shilpi Agarwal, Esq, Telephone 415-293-6321; Mollie M. Lee, Esq, Telephone 415-621-2493, ACLU Appearing for Plaintiff Senior and Disability Action et al;
Fred Nisen, Esq., Disabiity Rights Caifornia, Telephone 510-267-1264, appearing for Plaintiff Senior and Disability Action et al;
S. Michelle Inan, Esq., Paul Stein, Esq., State of California DOJ, Telephone 415-510-3802, appearing for Defendant Alex Padillas Secretary of State of California;

Matter is specially set for hearing at 1:30 p.m. on February 22, 2019 in Department 302 by Judge Ethan P. Schulman.

The Court address counsel with a number of questions that are of concern to the Court.

The Court discloses to counsel that he formerly served as a member of the board of directors of the A.C.L.U. of northern California which is one of the plaintiffs and Petitioners in this case; I was also a member and chair of the legal committee of that organization.
And also served as a coop rating attorney with the A.C.L.U. of northern California, before being appointed to the bench.

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Petitioners Senior And Disability Action, Alice Chiu, American Civil Liberties Union Of Northern'S Motion For A Peremptory Writ Of Mandate. (Specially Set At 1:30 Pm Before Judge Schulman)

The Court proposes to receive competing statements of decision from both counsel (draft statements of decision) and to use them as a starting point for issuing a written ruling.
It would be very useful to the Court if both counsel could submit to the Court proposed statements of decision addressing the key issues on which the resolution of the petition turns, and citing the key authorities that parties rely upon in that regard. The Court proposes to review both competing statements of decision, draft statements of decision and to use them as a starting point for issuing a written ruling. The statements of decision should address the principle material issues here that are before the Court for principal resolution. These are to be submitted simultaneously and in Word version by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org these are to be received by March 8, 2019, at which time this will be then considered taken under submission. (See Court Reporter transcripts for complete details)

Judge: Etahn P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Feb-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONERS SENIOR AND DISABILITY ACTION, ALICE CHIU, AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERTIES UNION OF NORTHERN'S Motion For A Peremptory Writ Of Mandate.

Judge Schulman discloses that he is a former member of the board of directors and former member of the Legal Committee of the American Civil Liberties Union of Northern California. This will not affect his ability to fairly and impartially decide this matter.

The hearing is specially set for Friday, February 22 at 1:30 p.m. If that date and time is not convenient for counsel, the parties may request the clerk to set the matter on another date at 1:30 p.m.

Counsel should be prepared at the hearing to address the following questions:

1. Is state mandamus relief available to compel Respondent to perform a ministerial duty arising under federal law?
2. In view of the express private right of action provision of the National Voter Registration Act (52 U.S.C. 20510(b)(2)), does a state court have concurrent jurisdiction over an action for an alleged violation of the Act, or is Petitioners' exclusive remedy a civil action in federal court for declaratory or injunctive relief?
3. If a state court does have concurrent jurisdiction over an alleged violation of the NVRA, does the 90-day written notice of violation prerequisite to suit provision of the Act ( 20510(b)) apply to such an action?
4. If the Court concludes that the 90-day provision applies, is dismissal the appropriate remedy, given that more than 90 days elapsed between Petitioners' June 20, 2018 prelitigation demand letter and the filing of Respondent Secretary of State's brief on the merits on January 14, 2019?
5. The Secretary has represented that he is "continuing his deliberate process of considering additional offices for designation as voter registration agencies, including the offices recommended by the ACLU and DRC," and has argued that for this reason, the action is not ripe for adjudication. What is the nature and substance of that process, and when does the Secretary anticipate completing it?

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

payments.description: REQ OR STIP AND ORDER W/O HEARING
